scope.target: Create one canonical repository pattern execution contract
scope.dependency_radius: Architecture, command/query handlers, persistence adapters, profiles, tests, outbox, and code review guidance
constraints: Preserve DDD, Clean Architecture, CQRS, and Hexagonal boundaries
Do not invent concrete Wolverine APIs
Keep canonical normative rules out of tutorial examples
Do not require a database, ORM, or persistence package
Preserve IDomainRepository as a validated compatibility contract derived from IAggregateRepository
Do not redefine the Use Case and Handler relationship in this task
Do not publish Aggregate batch persistence as a default building-block dependency
non_goals: No broad guide or example synchronization in this task

user_constraints: Do not proceed until decision questions are answered

steps: Update architecture and technology ownership statements
Define aggregate persistence, pure query, and capability-specific writer ports
Define repository port contract and adapter responsibilities
Define soft deletion, restricted physical purge, concurrency, explicit strong-consistency Unit of Work, outbox, and event lifecycle
Define an optional target-repository Aggregate batch capability pattern, opt-in evidence, and transaction/failure semantics without publishing a mandatory portable batch interface if D3 approves it
Move technology choices from universal requirements to conditional implementation guidance
validation: Search for contradictory abstraction names and method counts
Search for EF Core command-side requirements that violate the approved stack
Check project structure against port and adapter placement
Run git diff --check and changed-link validation
deferred_items: Dependent prompt, guide, example, and check synchronization
Use Case, Handler, Controller, and synchronous invocation realignment
